records.ety: [Scot. wraith, warth; probably originally, a guardian angel, from Icel. vörðr a warden, guardian, akin to E. ward. See Ward a guard.]

defs: An apparition of a person in his exact likeness, seen before death, or a little after; hence, an apparition; a specter; a vision; an unreal image.
quotes: She was uncertain if it were the gypsy or her wraith.
O, hollow wraith of dying fame.

defs: Sometimes, improperly, a spirit thought to preside over the waters; -- called also water wraith.
